text: Rename split (Pass A of the DEC-13/14 plan): `makemigrations` produced a destructive `DeleteModel`/`CreateModel`+`RemoveField`/`AddField` because renaming the model AND its targeting FK fields in one autodetector run defeats both heuristics; drop/create is unacceptable (seeded school rows). Chosen path (plan-approved): hand-author the rename ops (`RenameModel`+`RenameField`) and let Django generate the non-rename residual (AlterField/AlterModelOptions/constraint). First repo use of `RenameModel`/`RenameField` (no anti-precedent; the one prior "rename", school/0020, was a data-value change).

text: IMPL surfaced a spec deficiency in the orchestrator directive (mandate-008, stopped not guessed): the test gate runs SQLite (`config/settings/test.py`), where `RenameField` remakes the whole table and replays the still-present `assignment_target_matches_kind` CheckConstraint whose `Q` still referenced `department` → `FieldError` building the test DB (Postgres `RENAME COLUMN` would cascade; SQLite does not). Fix: `RemoveConstraint(assignment_target_matches_kind)` ordered as the first op in plans/0022 (ahead of the rename); the generated plans/0023 re-adds it with the `division` predicate. Harmless on Postgres.

text: Slice 8 (US-ONB-2 first-login set-password) exposes a security fork the approved blueprint under-specified: the blueprint's "show the activation/set-password link on-screen at signup_done" is unsafe on a PUBLIC endpoint — displaying a set-password token for an entered email either reveals account existence (enumeration) or, for an existing account, hands an attacker a password-set link (account takeover). Secure delivery of a set-password token to an anonymous user requires email (proves ownership), OR the flow must set the password in-session, OR self-service is dropped in favor of admin-distributed links (CSV path only). Surfaced to user for a decision before building Slice 8; Slice 7 (account genesis) stands.

Secure delivery of a set-password token to an anonymous user requires email (proves ownership), OR the flow must set the password in-session, OR self-service is dropped in favor of admin-distributed links (CSV path only). Surfaced to user for a decision before building Slice 8; Slice 7 (account genesis) stands.

text: DEC-17 (user): "blocked" action steps = **stored, first-class, queryable state** (corrects an initial misread toward a derived dependency signal). A user records a **blocker (impediment)** with reason + who/when; "blocked" = an unresolved impediment; a user can list all currently-blocked steps. Chosen shape (user, over a bare boolean): a per-`ActionStep` **`ActionStepImpediment`** child (reason translated; `raised_by/at` + `resolved_by/at` server-stamped; paired-resolution CheckConstraint) — NOT a `status` value (orthogonal axis), NOT a boolean, NOT dependency-derived. Adopted as **US-ORG-20** (pending). Plumbing spec'd in seed-round DEC-17 (model + service raise/resolve + school-scoped editable admin + DISC-03 plans group-extension for the 3 groups; school_admin full-CRUD via 0036, no DISC-19 strip since it's an editable child not a view-only audit log). NOT YET BUILT — own plan→impl slice (buildable independently of the front-end; "list all blocked" = an admin filter). No code change this turn.

Chosen shape (user, over a bare boolean): a per-`ActionStep` **`ActionStepImpediment`** child (reason translated; `raised_by/at` + `resolved_by/at` server-stamped; paired-resolution CheckConstraint) — NOT a `status` value (orthogonal axis), NOT a boolean, NOT dependency-derived. Adopted as **US-ORG-20** (pending). Plumbing spec'd in seed-round DEC-17 (model + service raise/resolve + school-scoped editable admin + DISC-03 plans group-extension for the 3 groups; school_admin full-CRUD via 0036, no DISC-19 strip since it's an editable child not a view-only audit log). NOT YET BUILT — own plan→impl slice (buildable independently of the front-end; "list all blocked" = an admin filter). No code change this turn.

text: DEC-17 / US-ORG-20 built (plan→impl, user: "ground all mockups' blocked representation in code"). Applied plans/0030–0031 to dev DB; table verified.

text: DEC-18 (user, clarifying intent for `web/`): `web/canonical.html` is a **required output materialization** — the formal WASC schoolwide action-plan document, fixed audience (visiting committee / accreditors), fixed shape (WASC sample template) — NOT a prototype to align to the model. The model is the single source of truth; it must be rich enough to MATERIALIZE canonical (a report/export view emits that exact shape from stored data) AND to drive the staff creation/visualization views. Direction: model → rendering. So: canonical keeps its WASC-AUDIENCE vocabulary (e.g. "Department / Person(s) Responsible"; KPI chips from a target-kind `SuccessCriterion`) and is NOT relabeled to db field names; every datum it shows must be materializable, and where it is not the MODEL changes (gap recorded in `source-model-gaps.md`, mandate-007) — canonical is never edited down. DEC-16/DEC-17 were already instances. Corrects my earlier mis-frame (canonical-as-source-prototype-to-preserve/align-down). `web/working.html` + `web/wizard.html` = staff creation/visualization views → SAME treatment as `mockups/a–e` (align rendered language + capabilities to db terms). `web/data/plan-data.js` = shared sample/import source (canonical reads it too); existing prototype keys NOT renamed. Persisted: "Materialization principle" framing at head of `data/source-model-gaps.md` + DEC-18 in `data/seed-round-plan.md` + this line + STATE. No code change this turn.

text: DEC-19 (user think-out: "how to retain canonicity of web/canonical AND allow milestones + division additions that post-date the web/ files"). Resolution: **`web/data/plan-data.js` is to be made EXACTLY faithful to the Django modeling** — the JS stand-in for stored plan data, model-shaped (`divisions` parent/head; `Milestone` tier Plan→Milestone→Phase→ActionStep with per-milestone improvement_type/planning_method; actors division+optional person, Role retired; per-step status/assessment/communications/impediments; quantification as target-kind SuccessCriterion, no standalone KPI). Single model-faithful sample SoT. **`web/canonical.html` stays as it is as a VIEW** — rendered WASC output/shape held constant, does NOT bring in elements its shape doesn't display (projects model-faithful data DOWN to the fixed WASC document, omitting milestone tier / division hierarchy it doesn't surface). Operative reading: "stays as it is" = OUTPUT-frozen not byte-frozen — its renderer is updated to read model-faithful keys while preserving output, guarded by a golden-output check vs the WASC sample (`sources/`). The "original prototype" divergence record is pinned to the prototype's git SHA in source-model-gaps.md (git freezes prior state); plan-data.js evolves past it. working.html/wizard.html read the same data, show milestones/divisions natively. Reframe that dissolved the tension: **canonicity is a property of canonical's RENDERER (it emits the WASC shape), not of plan-data.js's vocabulary** — so the data can track the model while canonical's renderer keeps the shape. Builds on DEC-18; resolves the two working/wizard STOP-flags. Persisted: source-model-gaps.md head + DEC-19 in seed-round-plan.md + this line + STATE. NOT yet built — next is plan→impl. No code change this turn.

text: DEC-20 — post-model architecture orientation (user think-out: "what other canonical Django-universe elements to use, given the AI-assisted wizard plan-creation vision"; the zero-API/views posture so far was a deliberate FIRST FOCUS on data modeling, not a permanent exclusion). Captured WITHOUT prejudicing any direction. SETTLED PRINCIPLE (invariant, not a direction): the **model is the contract** — Django views, forms/formsets, the create-plan wizard, AND any AI author-assist all write through the SAME model invariants (CheckConstraint/clean()) + services.py; the AI gets NO privileged write path (it proposes data the same constraints validate; the seeded reference vocabularies are simultaneously its grounding and its guardrails) — this is why model-first was right. CAPTURED LANDSCAPE (reference, not a selection): request/response layer (views, ModelForm + inline formsets, formtools SessionWizardView or HTMX wizard; draft state = lifecycle="proposed" + advance_plan_status gate); AI-specific (background tasks [Django 6.0 django.tasks or Celery/django-q] to move the slow LLM call off-request; async views + StreamingHttpResponse/SSE; an `ai` service module extending services.py; caching; env API key); cross-cutting (management commands for US-ONB-4 + AI batch; FileField/Storage for the open §H Evidence-payload gap; messages + permission mixins wiring existing groups); KEEP OUT per standing stances (signals for domain logic / no hooks; DRF/SPA unless a consumer appears — architect-review's "don't pre-build"; 2FA). EXPLICITLY UNCHOSEN: front-end direction (5 mockups are the input); every tech choice (CBV vs formtools vs HTMX; django.tasks vs Celery; SSE vs Channels; DRF vs none; whether to build the AI assist) OPEN. FLAGGED (not decided): the AI call's slowness forces off-request work → the background-task + async-streaming path shapes settings/deploy (a worker) + the wizard's interaction model, worth deciding early when a direction is taken. Persisted: DEC-20 in data/seed-round-plan.md + this line + STATE. No code this turn.

text: DEC-21 — reuse the LLM service elements from `django-llm-template` (gh:davidorex/django-llm-template; local /Users/david/Projects/digitalocean/django-llm-template/template/core/) for the AI author-assist. Same-author Copier template; assessed a strong HARVEST fit (not whole-template adoption — this project already exists, diverged on auth [custom onboarding] + API [no DRF]). LIKELY NEXT BUILD; audit/config/template elements land as Django MODELS here. Decoupled from Celery/Redis (service layer is infra-independent; wizard's natural path = streaming async+SSE, no extra infra; a worker is a separate later choice, and django.tasks-on-Postgres avoids Redis). Narrows DEC-20's AI-service piece (source chosen) without prejudicing front-end/Celery-vs-django.tasks/whether-wizard-ships. REUSE INVENTORY: (1) LLM service layer core/services/llm/ (get_llm/get_llm_logged; base.py LLMProvider/LLMResponse; providers anthropic/openai/openrouter/deepseek/openai_compat; .complete()/.stream(); httpx, no heavy SDK); (2) MODELS from core/models/llm.py — LLMProviderConfig (admin-editable runtime config), PromptTemplate (reusable, var substitution, sanitizes on render), LLMCall (audit log of every call — fits audit culture); (3) prompt_sanitizer.py (OWASP LLM01 injection defense); (4) settings shape LLM_API_KEYS/LLM_DEFAULT_PROVIDER/MODEL/TIMEOUT env-driven, default Anthropic claude-sonnet-4.5; (5) optional/deferred — SSE streaming util (when wizard streams) + Celery shared_task pattern (only if a worker is later chosen, else django.tasks). NOT reused: allauth (conflicts built onboarding), DRF stack (no-API), crispy-forms, the scaffold. ADAPTATION: dedicated ai/llm app; fit base-model/Meta; English-only (no modeltranslation); decide multi-school tenancy on LLMCall/PromptTemplate; admin/perms app-admin/superuser only (not school_admin); wire PromptTemplate grounding to seeded reference vocabularies (DEC-20). text: Comprehensive code-grounded model-surface audit (user: "deeply grounded investigation, not memory alone") → `docs/model-surface-audit.md` (519 lines; 48 concrete models + 2 abstract bases across users/school/accreditation/plans/ai). Full field/FK/M2M/constraint/Meta inventory + relationship graph + temporal/actor/resource surfaces + services + a candidate-view readiness table. READY: Global, All-plans-progress, All-plans-blockers (US-ORG-20). DERIVED (join, no single relation): Division (union of *_division FKs + unit tree), Calendar (fixed dates only), dependency-conflict (cross_plan_ref is an unresolved "<plan>:<step>" string, not an FK), schedule-overlap (fixed dates only). BLOCKED (missing capability): Position view (no ActionStep↔Position link; only Holding→Person→Assignment.person), goal/semantic conflict (no conflict model), overbooking (no capacity on Division/Person). AI-resolution = advisory/read-only (can ground on the subtree; no write-back surface). The four flagged gap-hypotheses ALL CONFIRMED TRUE: (i) no capacity/availability + RequiredResource is kind+free-note (no qty/unit); (ii) no RRULE — recurrence = kind + Frequency(code/label, no interval) + note; (iii) no conflict concept; (iv) no direct ActionStep↔Position. Extra gaps found: Evidence has no payload/file/URL; cross_plan_ref no referential integrity; several "uniqueness" guarantees are seed-convention not DB constraints; Division/Phase same-school/plan rules are clean()-only; no Proposal/KPI; ai.LLMCall has no plan/step/school link. **Code-vs-doc discrepancies flagged (mandate-008, for user decision — NOT auto-reconciled):** `data/source-model-gaps.md` still carries retired Department/Role refs + a stale Evidence-owner "exactly-one-of department/role" / "Role.department required FK" that don't match current code (Evidence.owner_division required + owner_person optional); `docs/domain-model.md` predates the Phase-14 Plan primitives. Read-only investigation; no model/app code changed.

text: DEC-23 — IA/visibility INITIAL ENVISIONING persisted (user: "persist these IA/visibility principles as an initial envisioning") → `docs/ia-visibility-envisioning.md`, the behavior/visibility companion to FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS.md (structure/naming). Status: design intent, NOT binding; refinable; front-end direction unchosen. Resolving idea: default to relevance, full set one step away; "when" = pull/in-view ranking (NO push — notifications excluded), "as" = altitude-appropriate granularity. Three altitudes (Role retired): Division/Position/Person. Principles: progressive disclosure/drill-don't-dump; exception-first (blocked/overdue/stalled/overbooked/due-soon/newly-assigned); time-horizon Now·Next·Later; altitude density (head=signals, holder=seat tasks, person=detail); one consolidated home per person faceted by altitude (US-ORG-15/16); pull-not-push. Per-altitude: Division=roll-up+exceptions drilling the parent tree (US-ORG-12/19; d-dashboard prototype); Position=one seat-faceted worklist (needs Assignment.position); Person=consolidated my-work. Enablers ready (rollup/blocked/fixed-date/stalled-via-transition-`at`) vs gated by source-model-gaps §I (Assignment.position; capacity for overbooked; RRULE for recurrence). Constraints baked: no notifications, no KPIs; step actions write through services+constraints (DEC-20). Docs only; no code.

Flags: ES-module sibling imports stay relative (preserve per-suite js layout); plan-data.js shared classic OUTSIDE suite dirs (static/shared); canonical.html outside the switcher. Not built — NEXT: user chooses which 2–3 mockups become candidate suites, then build = own plan→impl. Docs only.

text: DEC-26 — FRONT-END BUILD GREENLIT (user: "i'm fine with starting front end cuz i can be all user positions"). text: DEC-27 — plan-creation front-end architecture decided; IMPL in flight. New `planner` app: SHARED canonical Django forms (ModelForms + nested inlineformset_factory + per-kind clean() mirroring the Assignment/Timeline/SuccessCriterion/FeedbackChannel/Communication/ReviewEvent CheckConstraints + school-scoped FK querysets) + a shared single-page create view; a "suite" = presentation skin only (template inheritance + per-suite static), first suite `wizard` skinned from mockups/e-wizard (recomposability heuristic: forms decided once, not per suite). Multi-step mechanism = **Option C** (user choice over formtools/server-stepper): one server-rendered page with all forms/formsets, e-suite ES-module JS paginates client-side (progressive enhancement, works without JS), ONE atomic POST creates the Plan graph (author/school stamped, lifecycle=proposed). Minimal but extensible (steps.py registry + shared partials + template blocks → steps/fields/suites/promote/draft-resume all additive). django-formtools rejected (can't model nested action-step formsets; commits-at-end vs durable draft). Capture = plain ORM, zero services (gate runs only at advance-to-Active). Scope first cut = full e-step capture → save Proposed (US-1..21); resolves US-UI-1 (structured Timeline). Deferred (additive): promote-to-Active (US-22), durable mid-way draft, suite-selector (DEC-24), ActionStepDependency capture, Position-as-responsible (§I), Evidence payload (§H.1). App-owned templates/<suite>/+static/<suite>/ (APP_DIRS, zero settings churn); suite prefix hardcoded `wizard/`. Plan ~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md. Dispatching IMPL subagent. Docs only this commit.

text: The `planner` data-readiness audit found the wizard school-scopes its dependent selects but does NOT narrow/validate the dependency: `planning_method` is not filtered by the chosen milestone `improvement_type` (`PlanningMethod.applicable_improvement_types`), and `person` is not filtered by the chosen assignment `division`; the promotion gate enforces only `requires_planning_method`, not applicability — so an author can pick/POST an incompatible combination. (All required selects ARE populated with real seeded data; the only empty source is `school.Person` [0 rows] feeding the 4 OPTIONAL person pickers — known-pending roster/D7, not a defect.) Captured as **US-UI-2** (create-plan interface fidelity): dependent selects narrow to valid options (client-side progressive enhancement) AND the choice is validated server-side in the form `clean()` (so non-JS/crafted POSTs can't bypass; mirrors DEC-20 + the planner's per-kind clean() mirrors). improvement_type↔planning_method has data (4 methods, 5 links); person↔division derives via Holding/Position + Division.head and is moot until the Person roster seeds. Persisted: `phases/00-preamble.md` (US-UI section) + `phases/US-STATUS.md` (US-UI-2 pending) + this line. Piping through the operation process per user: now → plan mode → explore → write plan → impl.

text: US-UI-2 BUILT (`6c5249e`): dependent-select narrowing + server-side validation in the `planner` wizard. (1) improvement_type→planning_method (MilestoneForm): __init__ narrows planning_method to applicable methods + clean() friendly error; **empty `applicable_improvement_types` = UNCONSTRAINED** (applicable set = linked ∪ no-links). (2) division→person for ALL person pickers (Assignment.person, owner_person on FeedbackChannel/Communication, responsible_person on ReviewEvent) via a shared `DivisionPersonNarrowingMixin`: __init__ narrows to `Division.people_in_subtree()` + clean() friendly error. New reusable `Division.people_in_subtree()` (school/models/org.py, code-only, no migration) = subtree(D+descendants) holders (Holding→Position.division) + heads. Client narrowing: `_build_dependency_maps()` (view) → `{{ …|json_script:"planner-dependency-maps" }}` (CSP-safe) → `dependents.js` disables non-applicable child options per nearest .planner-fs-row, resets if invalidated (progressive enhancement; server is the backstop, page works JS-off). The __init__ queryset narrowing is the real ModelChoiceField backstop (rejects out-of-set on bound POST). division→person is correct-but-moot (0 Person rows; D7). **Orchestrator independent verify:** scope = planner/ + school/models/org.py + school/tests/ only; mypy 236; makemigrations --check clean (no migration); pytest 745 (+25); ruff baseline-only; node --check JS ok; org.py imports used (Pyright "Q/Person not accessed" = LSP false positives). **US-UI-2 → enabled.** Established semantics persisted to domain-model.md (PlanningMethod empty=unconstrained; Division.people_in_subtree membership derivation). Tree clean.

text: devmocks DEBUG-only mock-launcher BUILT (`4f68cb1`); plan ~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md. User-approved consolidation of the two static UI-mock sets (repo-root `mockups/` a–e + `web/` canonical/working/wizard/index) to run off `manage.py runserver` under one launcher. New self-contained `devmocks` app (no models): LauncherView (TemplateView) at `/mocks/` + two `django.views.static.serve` re_paths scoped to `BASE_DIR.parent/"mockups"` and `…/"web"` (per-set path prefix preserves each page's relative asset + `data/plan-data.js` refs); normalized two-section card launcher (templates/devmocks/launcher.html + static/devmocks/launcher.css ported from mockups/index.css). Wired: `devmocks` in INSTALLED_APPS after planner; `config/urls.py` DEBUG-gated `path("mocks/", include("devmocks.urls"))` OUTSIDE i18n_patterns (net: /mocks/, /mocks/mockups/<path>, /mocks/web/<path>). The two file-launchers (mockups/index.html, web/index.html) left intact; CLAUDE.md preview note → runserver `/mocks/` primary. **CSP (user path (a)):** the app's global CSP (`default-src 'self'`) attaches to served pages and would block the mocks' JS-applied inline `style="…"` + canonical's inline `<script>`, rendering them unstyled — so the two serve routes are wrapped `csp_exempt()` (django-csp 4.x factory), scoped to the DEBUG-only /mocks/ serve routes; launcher view keeps CSP. **Orchestrator independent verify (runserver smoke; routes are DEBUG-gated so unreachable under config.settings.test → no pytest surface):** CSP header ABSENT on /mocks/mockups|web/… (exempt ✓), PRESENT on /mocks/ launcher + real routes (control ✓); every page 200 + correct MIME (module `.js` = text/javascript); headless Chrome renders module pages + canonical with inline styles applied (c-kanban 7, canonical 40 inline-style attrs; canonical inline script runs). Static gate: check 0; makemigrations --check clean (no models); ruff devmocks/ clean + format; mypy 240 (+4); pytest 745 (unchanged). Scope = devmocks/ (new) + config/{settings/base,urls}.py + CLAUDE.md. No US (dev review harness, not an author-facing capability). No DEC (operational tooling, not a domain/architecture decision; CSP carve-out rationale captured in devmocks/urls.py comment + this line). Tree clean.

text: Claude Design (`claude.ai/design`, Anthropic Labs preview) external-tool evaluation captured to **`docs/claude-design-research.md`** (new). Three online-research passes consolidated: (1) general identity/access/IO/pricing/quotas/data-handling + fit-for-this-repo; (2) empirical probe of the export's CSP-relevant structure; (3) probe of repo/branch/worktree ingestion. Confidence-tagged throughout ([CONFIRMED]/[INFERRED]/[UNVERIFIED]); central caveat recorded — NO public byte-for-byte `claude.ai/design` standalone export exists, so §4 export-structure findings are INFERRED from Anthropic's frontend-aesthetics cookbook system prompt + adjacent real artifacts, not the literal export. Established facts: Claude Design = browser-only (Opus 4.7) visual-design workspace, paid-only, separate weekly quota (tight on Pro), no data-residency/audit-logs; inputs incl. linked GitHub/GitLab repo + local-folder drag-drop + WebGrabber live-DOM capture; outputs standalone HTML/ZIP/PDF/PPTX/share-URL + a "Hand off to Claude Code (local|Web)" bundle. Export default is **CSP-hostile** vs this repo's `default-src 'self'` (inline `<style>`/`<script>` blocks + CDN Tailwind `<script>` + Google-Fonts `<link>`) → a transform layer is ALWAYS required; heavy-per-cycle by default, reducible to mechanical (externalize blocks to `.css`/`.js` matching FRONTEND-CONVENTIONS, vendor/drop CDN, self-host fonts) ONLY if the generation prompt is locked CSP-friendly (durability across sessions UNVERIFIED). Repo ingest uses the GitHub-App connector (manual "Sync now" snapshot, likely main-only — branch selector UNVERIFIED for Design); **worktree-folder drag-drop = deterministic branch-state pin** (git-unaware filesystem read), fits this project's worktree workflow; `Evilander/claude-design-mcp` is NOT a handle on claude.ai/design. The "refine+finalize the suite in Claude Design" framing FITS the repo's existing materialization pattern (substitutes the hand-authored mockup layer; committed export = design-of-record per DEC-18/19; suite=skin per DEC-25/27) given a re-entry loop + a stable token-interface conversion contract; bilingual stays unfinalizable in Design (EN-only; 中文 Django-side). **DECISION OPEN — no DEC, no adoption.** Gating pre-adoption action (mandate-004, the one unresolved fragility): user generates ONE real export from their account + inspects it against the app CSP (agents cannot reach claude.ai/design). STATE open-decisions + this line; US n/a. Docs-only commit.

Assists: A1/A1b narrative prose (+zh-hans), A2 WASC-standard alignment, A3 domain M2M selection, A4 priority-deviation rationale, B1 typed success criteria, B2 channel binding, C1 feedback channels, D1 milestones+improvement-type+method, E1 phases, F1 flagship method-recipe→action-steps (uses seeded `PlanningStep.template`), F2 assignments, F3 structured timeline, F4 resources+substeps, F5 evidence (no re-entry path yet), G1 whole review-loop (index-ref discipline), H1 read-only coherence critique, H2 orchestrated whole-draft. text: Create-page pulldown audit (user: "the cycle pulldown is empty; check all the pulldowns"). Findings: (1) the empty cycle the user saw = the logged-in superuser `davidenchine@outlook.com` has `User.school=None` → ALL school-scoped dropdowns collapse to empty + the assist endpoint 403s; operational fix = set the user's `school` to `chiway-repton-xiamen` (verified: for the tenant school every PlanForm dropdown has data — cycle 2, framing 4, priority 3, M2Ms 3–7, improvement_type 32, planning_method 4, standards 12, division 10, stakeholder 6, frequency 4). (2) Person pickers empty for the tenant too = D7 roster unseeded (known §I gap). (3) **Real create-UI gap → DISC-20-0525-A + US-UI-5:** `Phase.milestone`, `ActionStep.phase`, and the `milestone` FK on FeedbackChannel/Communication/ReviewEvent are plain FK selects scoped to the plan's existing rows (empty on a single-page create); `save_nested` index-resolves ONLY the FeedbackChannel/ReviewEvent refs (`channel_index`/`trigger_index`), not these hierarchy/timing links — so the `Plan→Milestone→Phase→ActionStep` hierarchy (US-17, US-ext-milestone) and milestone-anchored timing (`timing_kind=milestone`) are NOT capturable in the create UI (FKs optional → flat creation works; `timing_kind=milestone` is a dead-end, empty select → `_clean_timing` unsatisfiable). Resolution = extend the create-order-index mechanism to the milestone/phase parents (or dynamic select population); parallels US-UI-3/4. text: User added a draft-review capability: during the draft phase (lifecycle=proposed), a set of school users ROUTED BY the plan's type/recipe may COMMENT ON THE DRAFT itself (stakeholder feedback on the draft, to inform the admin before the US-UI-6 flip). Terminology pinned for the user ("type"=`ImprovementType`, "recipe"=`PlanningMethod`; both per-`Milestone`, DEC-14). DISTINCT from the plan's operational FeedbackChannel/Communication/ReviewEvent (execution-time) — this is a review/comment layer over the draft. Thought-out in conversation; 4 forks confirmed via AskUserQuestion: (1) routing = a configured ImprovementType/PlanningMethod→audience mapping (`DraftReviewerRule`), a draft's commenters = the UNION across its milestones' type/recipe rules (not per-draft selection, not a new plan-level kind — DEC-14 stands); (2) reviewer identity = ORG STRUCTURE (Position/Division holders via Holding/Person.user); (3) comment scope = PLAN + ELEMENTS (a `DraftComment` targets the plan OR a child — action step/milestone/criterion/etc.; generic target); (4) ADVISORY ONLY — does not gate the flip (DEC-29 unchanged, admin alone flips), no acknowledgment/resolution workflow (exclusion). New models needed (own plan→impl, NOT built): `DraftComment` (FK Plan + generic target + author + body + created-at; allowed while proposed) and `DraftReviewerRule` (type/recipe→Position/Division audience). Atomic stories, `user`-actor only (no `llm-user`): US-REV-1 (admin configures the rule), US-REV-2 (reviewer comments, plan/element), US-REV-3 (author/admin sees comments while reviewing the draft; advisory, non-gating), US-REV-4 (reviewer pull-discovers drafts-to-review). Dependency: live commenting needs the Person roster + User links (D7) — mapping+models build/test independently of D7. Exclusions held: no notifications (pull, DEC-23 IA), no ack/resolution (advisory), no KPIs, no hooks; model-is-contract preserved (comments are human content; admin remains the sole lifecycle gate; no llm-user added — not stated by the user). Persisted: DEC-30 (data/seed-round-plan.md) + `phases/00-preamble.md` "Draft review — stakeholder commenting (US-REV)" section + `phases/US-STATUS.md` (US-REV-1..4 pending) + STATE block. Not built; analysis/articulation only. Docs-only commit.

text: US-UI-5 (same-session milestone/phase linking) BUILT (`3aa780b`) — US-UI-5 → enabled; DISC-20-0525-A resolved. Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent; no forks (the index-ref pattern + progressive-enhancement convention dictated every choice). IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus): the five plan-local FKs (`Phase.milestone`, `ActionStep.phase`, `FeedbackChannel`/`Communication`/`ReviewEvent.milestone`) converted to **form-only `*_index` IntegerFields** (Select widget, JS-populated) — dropped from the forms' `Meta.fields`; resolved server-side in `save_nested` via `_resolve_index` against new `milestones_by_index`/`phases_by_index` (captured right after milestones/phases save; order already correct). `_save_plan_owned` gained an optional `link_resolver` callback; `_optional_link_resolver` (nullable Phase.milestone/ActionStep.phase → None on blank/out-of-range) + `_milestone_timing_link_resolver` (raises `ValidationError` when a `timing_kind=milestone` row's index doesn't resolve). `_clean_timing` (FeedbackChannel/Communication) + `ReviewEventForm.clean()` read `*_index`. View wraps the atomic save in try/except ValidationError → rollback + non-field error + HTTP 200 re-render (no DB IntegrityError). New `milestone-phasing.js` (CSP-safe ES module, imported by main.js) populates each `*_index` select from surviving milestone/phase row labels (value=create-order index, text=label), refreshing on label input + a new `planner:formset-changed` event dispatched by `formsets.js`; `kindgate.js` now gates `milestone_index`. **No model change → no migration** (makemigrations --check clean). Progressive enhancement preserved: no-JS → blank index selects → optional links unset (flat plan still creatable), milestone-timing needs JS. **Orchestrator independent verify:** check 0; makemigrations --check NO CHANGES; ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; planner/ clean); ruff format clean; mypy **251**; pytest **791** (+5 new in test_milestone_phase_linking: index→FK resolution for all five, blank=no-link, out-of-range optional=no-link, milestone-timed bad/missing index=form error not 500); node --check all JS ok. No DB migration to apply. Pyright "not accessed" = documented false positives (mypy is the gate). DEC-29 closure: **US-UI-5 cleared** (the second present create-UI gap) → US-LLM-15 (phases assist) unblocked; with US-UI-3 done, the wizard now captures the FULL promotable + hierarchical plan graph. Tracking: US-STATUS US-UI-5 → enabled; discoveries DISC-20-0525-A resolved_by; STATE refresh + this line; tracking commit. Tree clean.

text: US-LLM-16 (DEC-28 F1 — decompose a planning-method recipe into action steps) BUILT (`e83e437`) — US-LLM-16 → enabled; the 2nd real `AssistSpec`. Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent; two user-confirmed forks: (1) **the LLM proposes the fitting method** (no author picker, no foundation param extension — grounding carries ALL methods + their ordered templates + the plan draft; the model selects + expands, bounded to provided methods), (2) **direct fill like A1** (`preview="fields"`). IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus): `planner/assist.py` — new `AssistResult(prefill, note=None)` dataclass + `parse_decompose_action_steps` (validates `{"method","steps":[{description,assessment}]}`, returns the steps list as prefill + a note naming the chosen recipe; raises on malformed/empty) + the F1 `AssistSpec` (key=`decompose-action-steps`, target_step=`steps`, preview=`fields`, grounding_include = school/cycle/improvement_types/planning_methods/areas_for_improvement/learner_outcomes/draft_state) registered idempotently; `planner/views.py` — `AssistStreamView` normalizes `AssistResult` → `{prefill, note}` in the result frame (bare return → `{prefill}`, A1 back-compat); data migration `ai/0004_seed_decompose_action_steps_template` (idempotent get_or_create, the F1 PromptTemplate w/ the strict JSON contract; EN-only; "choose only from provided methods, don't invent methodology"); `ai-assist.js` — **add-then-fill for a list prefill** (the first formset-target assist: adds `steps` rows until count matches, fills by index, scoped to the target formset by `data-prefix` to avoid nested step-child rows) + shows the note in the status line; `formsets.js` — exported `addRow`/`addRows`. F1 produces step TEXT only (does NOT set Milestone.planning_method — advisory/surfaced); `phase_index` left blank (ungrouped; author groups via US-UI-5); the model-is-contract backstop is just `ActionStepForm`. **Orchestrator independent verify:** check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes (ai/0004 = data migration); ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; ai/+planner/ clean); ruff format clean; mypy **252**; pytest **811** (+20: test_f1_decompose 17 + A1 back-compat assertions); node --check ai-assist/formsets/main ok. **Migration `ai/0004` applied to dev DB** via superuser DSN — `decompose-action-steps` PromptTemplate present (3193-char body, method+steps contract; 2 templates total). A1 back-compat confirmed (its result frame carries a dict prefill + no note). Pyright "not accessed" = documented false positives (mypy is the gate). DEC-29 progress: **first per-element assist (F1) landed** atop the now-complete capture surface (US-UI-3/5); the reusable `AssistResult` note channel is now available to later assists. Browser pass (human, provider key) folds with the US-LLM-6 pass. Tracking: US-STATUS US-LLM-16 → enabled; STATE gate anchor + this line; tracking commit. Tree clean.

+ `label=` on all 6 specs), `planner/assist.py` (`AssistSpec.label` field), `planner/views.py` (serialize label), `planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js` (whole-form `collectDraftState`; label button text; new `applyBindingPrefill` for `preview="bindings"` — navigates criteria row i → its measurement-0 → channel formset, `addRows` + sets `channel_index` per binding), `planner/templates/planner/_assist.html` (button text = label), `planner/templates/planner/_formset.html` (added `data-prefix` to the nested `.planner-nested` wrappers so the binding navigation + `addRows` reach nested formsets — the in-build deviation, minimal + sound), data migration `ai/0008_seed_bind_measurement_channels_template`, `planner/tests/test_b2_binding.py` (31 tests). **Output** `{"bindings":[{criterion_index,channel_indices:[int]}]}` (create-order indices; out-of-range dropped at save by `_resolve_index`). **Two reusable bits B2 forced:** (1) **whole-form `draft_state`** — `collectDraftState` now sends the entire wizard form, not just the control's step, so cross-section assists work AND every prior assist now grounds on the full draft (fixes a latent per-step under-grounding, e.g. B1 not seeing basics' desired_state — masked by the fake-provider tests + no live browser pass yet; mandate-007 surfaced+fixed); (2) **per-spec `AssistSpec.label`** (the criteria step now has 2 controls — B1+B2 — needing distinct buttons). **Orchestrator independent verify:** check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes (ai/0008 = data migration); ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; ai/+planner/ clean); ruff format clean; mypy **256**; pytest **937** (+31 B2; A1/B1/F1/D1/C1 green incl. an explicit unaffected-by-B2 assertion); node --check ai-assist/formsets/main ok; tree clean. **Migration `ai/0008` applied to dev DB** via superuser DSN — `bind-measurement-channels` PromptTemplate present (2706-char body, contract keys; **6 templates total**). Pyright "not accessed" (incl. `_TARGET_FIELDS`) = documented false positives (mypy is the gate). DEC-29 progress: 6 per-element assists built (A1/F1/B1/D1/C1/B2); B2 completes the "criteria are measurable" gate predicate (criteria↔channels). Remaining per-element: A2/A3/A4/A1b, E1, F2/F3/F4/F5, G1; then the orchestration trio (US-LLM-24/25/23) + US-UI-6. text: US-LLM-19 (DEC-28 F4 — propose required resources + a sub-step decomposition per action step) BUILT (`db338ee`) — US-LLM-19 → enabled; the 9th real `AssistSpec`; 3rd per-step-nested-child assist and the FIRST to fill TWO child types per step + the FIRST to fill LISTS (multi-row, not just row 0). Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent; NO user fork — built both halves of the envisioned F4 (US-16e resources + US-16g substeps) because substeps add a near-zero-cost second text-list under the same navigator (no meaningful scope tradeoff, unlike F2's responsible-only fork). IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus), 4 files: `planner/specs.py` (`parse_propose_step_resources` → AssistResult rows + "Proposed resources & sub-steps for N steps" note; `_RESOURCE_KINDS` frozenset mirrors `ResourceKind.values`; F4 spec key=`propose-step-resources`, target_step=`steps`, preview=`resources-substeps`, grounding=school/draft_state, NO resolve, label "Propose resources & sub-steps"; registered idempotently), `planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js` (`applyResourceSubstepPrefill` reusing `stepChildFormset(_,i,"resource"/"substep")` + `addRows`/`directRows` to grow EACH segment to the proposed row count then fill kind+note / description per row; skips empty substep block + out-of-range step + absent formset; thin `runResourceSubstepAssist` + the `preview="resources-substeps"` route + wireControl error-branch), data migration `ai/0011_seed_propose_step_resources_template` (dep ai/0010, no-op reverse, idempotent get_or_create; the five `ResourceKind` values stated inline with meanings; strict one-key OUTPUT CONTRACT), `planner/tests/test_f4_resources_substeps.py` (36 tests). **Output** `{"steps":[{step_index,resources:[{kind,note}],substeps:[str]}]}` — resources NON-EMPTY per step (parse STRICTLY rejects a `resources:[]` step since it would fail the gate), substeps optional; `note` default ""; `SubStep.order` = list position (assigned in `_save_actionstep_children`). **No model/form/formset/schema change** — RequiredResource/SubStep models+forms + the "resource"/"substep" segments in `ACTIONSTEP_CHILD_FORMSETS` + `_save_actionstep_children` (binds action_step, assigns order) + `_attach_nested_for_render` all pre-existed. EN-only (note/description filled; description_zh_hans blank). **Orchestrator independent verify:** check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes (ai/0011 = data migration); ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; ai/+planner/ clean); ruff format clean (352 files); mypy **259**; pytest **1041** (+36 F4; A1/B1/F1/D1/C1/B2/F2/F3 green); node --check ai-assist/formsets/main ok; tree clean. **Migration `ai/0011` applied to dev DB** via superuser DSN — `propose-step-resources` PromptTemplate present (**9 templates total**: bind-measurement-channels/decompose-action-steps/draft-success-criteria/plan-narrative-draft/propose-assignments/propose-milestones/propose-step-resources/propose-timelines/suggest-feedback-channels). Pyright "not accessed" (incl. `_TARGET_FIELDS` + migration callable params + pytest fixtures) = documented false positives (mypy is the gate). DEC-29 progress: 9 per-element assists (A1/F1/B1/D1/C1/B2/F2/F3/F4); the per-step gate predicates now covered for assignment+timeline+resource — **F5 (evidence) is the last per-step gate element** (blocked on US-UI-3's per-step Evidence sub-formset, now built, + a division resolve). After F5: A2 (standards)/A3 (domain M2Ms — introduces multi-select prefill)/G1 (review loop), then the orchestration trio (US-LLM-24/25/23) + US-UI-6. Tracking: US-STATUS US-LLM-19 → enabled; STATE gate anchor + assist summary + this line; tracking commit. Tree clean.

text: US-LLM-20 (DEC-28 F5 — propose the evidence each action step will produce) BUILT (`d6d61d8`) — US-LLM-20 → enabled; the 10th real `AssistSpec` and the **LAST per-step gate element** (after F5 every per-step promotion-gate predicate — responsible assignment / timeline / ≥1 resource / ≥1 evidence — has an author-assist). Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent; NO user fork — fields settled by D7 (division-only, consistent with F2/C1). **F5 = a FUSION of two existing precedents:** F4's multi-row per-step list fill (`stepChildFormset`+`addRows`/`directRows`) AND F2's FK-vocab `resolve` (label→school-scoped pk, drop-unresolvable). IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus), 4 files: `planner/specs.py` (`parse_propose_evidence` → AssistResult rows + "Proposed evidence for N steps" note; strict — a `evidence:[]` step rejects the whole payload since it would fail the gate; `resolve_evidence_vocab` walks the NESTED steps→evidence structure mapping each owner_division label→Division pk and DROPPING an evidence row whose division does not resolve [owner_division required]; `F5_GROUNDING_INCLUDE=("school","divisions","draft_state")` asserted vs SECTION_KEYS; F5 spec key=`propose-evidence`, target_step=`steps`, preview=`evidence`, resolve=resolve_evidence_vocab, label "Propose evidence"; registered idempotently), `planner/static/wizard/js/ai-assist.js` (`applyEvidencePrefill` reusing `stepChildFormset(_,i,"evidence")` + `addRows`/`directRows` to grow the evidence sub-formset to the proposed count then fill label/owner_division[resolved pk, dispatch change]/location; thin `runEvidenceAssist` + the `preview="evidence"` route + wireControl error-branch), data migration `ai/0012_seed_propose_evidence_template` (dep ai/0011, no-op reverse, idempotent get_or_create; body reads draft steps + the division labels, instructs ≥1 evidence/step [label + owner_division by listed label + optional location], strict one-key OUTPUT CONTRACT, no person/status/fences), `planner/tests/test_f5_evidence.py` (38 tests). **Output** `{"steps":[{step_index,evidence:[{label,owner_division,location}]}]}` — evidence NON-EMPTY per step; owner_division a LABEL resolved server-side; location optional. **Evidence is a PLAIN formset** (plan-owned + step-linked via the `evidence_artifacts` M2M, not an ActionStep inline child) saved by `_save_actionstep_evidence` (server-stamps plan, M2M-links the step, skips blank rows); owner_person/status(PLANNED default)/retention_until/label_zh_hans all OMITTED (D7 division-only, EN-only). **Data-prefix gotcha (build step 3) did NOT apply** — IMPL confirmed `_formset.html` renders every `form.nested` entry uniformly as `.planner-nested[data-prefix]` and the view hangs `nested["evidence"]` on the same dict as the FK children, so the navigator reaches it identically to resource/substep; NO template change. **Orchestrator independent verify:** check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes (ai/0012 = data migration; no template edit needed); ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; ai/+planner/ clean); ruff format clean (354 files); mypy **260**; pytest **1079** (+38 F5; A1/B1/F1/D1/C1/B2/F2/F3/F4 green; the new resolve is F5-local); node --check ai-assist/formsets/main ok; tree clean. **Migration `ai/0012` applied to dev DB** via superuser DSN — `propose-evidence` PromptTemplate present (**10 templates total**: bind-measurement-channels/decompose-action-steps/draft-success-criteria/plan-narrative-draft/propose-assignments/propose-evidence/propose-milestones/propose-step-resources/propose-timelines/suggest-feedback-channels). Pyright "not accessed" (incl. `_TARGET_FIELDS` + migration callable params + pytest fixtures) = documented false positives (mypy is the gate). DEC-29 progress: **10 per-element assists (A1/F1/B1/D1/C1/B2/F2/F3/F4/F5); the per-step gate elements are now COMPLETE** (each step has assignment+timeline+resource+evidence assists). Remaining per-element: A2 (standards)/A3 (domain M2Ms — introduces multi-select prefill)/G1 (review loop); then the orchestration trio US-LLM-24 (heuristic) → US-LLM-25 (gap-closure) → US-LLM-23 (whole-draft) + US-UI-6 (admin promote on the plan surface). Tracking: US-STATUS US-LLM-20 → enabled; STATE gate anchor + assist summary + this line; tracking commit. Tree clean.

text: JS apply-layer behavioral test harness BUILT (`d30cd59`) + DEC-31 + DISC-21. User-directed "full pipeline treatment" of the gap I surfaced (the client apply layer was gated only by `node --check` syntax; the B2 `data-prefix` defect had escaped that + the Python tests, caught only by human reasoning). Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent (mapped the JS export surface + DOM contract + gate wiring); ONE user fork (DOM harness) → user directed an independent no-prejudice RESEARCH agent, which empirically probed jsdom/happy-dom/linkedom on Node v23.7.0 against the six exact behaviors running the real `formsets.js` → **jsdom@27.0.1 + node --test** (linkedom disqualified on `<select>.value` getter-only; zero-dep shim rejected as false-confidence; headless browser disproportionate). IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus), 13 files: `ai-assist.js` (test-only named `export` on the 9 apply/navigator fns; logic unchanged; `initAiAssist` stays boot entry), new `school-improvement-plans/package.json` (`"type":"module"` at the project root — blast-radius checked, every `.js` under root is a wizard ESM module, none regress; jsdom devDep; `test:js` script) + committed `package-lock.json` (force-added past a user-global lockfile ignore) + `node_modules/` gitignored, new `jstests/` (`dom-fixture.mjs` installer+builder + 7 `*.test.mjs` files = 25 tests covering the matrix incl. the B2-regression), `Makefile` `test-js` target folded into the aggregate. **Two IMPL deviations (both contract-driven, sound):** (a) `node --test 'jstests/**/*.test.mjs'` glob (Node 23 errors on a bare dir arg); (b) two fixture/assertion realities pinned — `reindexRow` reindexes `name` not `id_`-prefixed `id`/`for` (→ DISC-21), and nested fixtures start each segment at its rendered blank-extra (count 1) since `addRow` clones an existing row. **Deliberately-broken probe:** a reintroduced B2-style `-0-` hardcode in `stepChildFormset` made the navigator test FAIL (step-1 → null), then reverted (not committed) — proving the suite catches the historical defect class. **Orchestrator independent verify:** `make test-js` 25/25; manage.py check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes; ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing); ruff format clean (354); mypy **260**; pytest **1079** (unchanged — the only Python-adjacent change is JS exports); commit clean, lockfile+package.json tracked, 0 node_modules files tracked, tree clean. **DEC-31 recorded** (`data/seed-round-plan.md`) — the harness choice + research basis + jsdom@27.0.1/Node-23 pin + the first-npm-footprint (dev/test-only; browser runtime unchanged). **Gate definition updated** so future increments run it: `make test-js` added to `CLAUDE.md` workflow step 7, `phases/PHASE-LAUNCH-CHECKLIST.md` step 7, and `templates/impl-static.md` `<pre_commit_checks>` (each with the `npm ci`-once precondition since `node_modules/` is gitignored). **DISC-21-0525-A recorded** (the `reindexRow` id/for defect the harness surfaced — duplicate ids on added rows; affects every multi-row formset + the F4/F5 assists; assist prefill unaffected since apply selects by `[name$=]`; pre-existing; recorded OPEN as a small plan→impl, resolution direction = widen the regex to `^(id_)?(${prefix})-(\d+)-`; NOT fixed — user decides). No US-STATUS row (quality infrastructure, not a user story). Process note: paused mid-pass on a user "why are you making edits / are you implementing or adhering to role" challenge — confirmed all edits are orchestrator-owned tracking/governance docs (DEC log, discoveries, CLAUDE.md/checklist/IMPL-template gate definition, LOG, STATE), NOT the IMPL's code; user said "go on". Tracking: DEC-31 + DISC-21 + gate docs + this line + STATE; tracking commit. Tree clean.

text: DISC-21-0525-A RESOLVED (`7217045`) — `reindexRow` now reindexes cloned formset rows' `id`/`for`, not just `name`. User directed the fix as the next step ("nah. no deviation ever from canonical" — after I'd started substituting my own direct reads for the Explore phase; corrected → ran the full canonical loop). Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent (confirmed the regex correctness at top-level/nested/two-level, that nothing in the JS suite reads `id`/`for` so the fix is safe, that the fixture already emits `id_` ids, and that it's JS-only); no user fork. IMPL subagent (general-purpose/opus), 3 files: `planner/static/wizard/js/formsets.js` (`reindexRow` regex `^(${prefix})-(\d+)-` → `^(id_)?(${prefix})-(\d+)-`, replacement `$1-` → `$1$2-`; `$1` captures the optional `id_` [empty for `name`], `$2` the prefix; only the leading index segment rewrites so a nested row's own deeper index stays 0; `addRow`/`clearRow`/`managementInput` + the apply functions untouched), `jstests/add-rows.test.mjs` (flipped the prior pinned-bug id assertion `id_feedback-0-label`→`id_feedback-1-label` + rewrote its comment; added a `for`-reindex test + a nested-row id-reindex test [`steps` parent clone → `id_steps-1-resource-0-note`, nested own `-0-` intact]), `jstests/dom-fixture.mjs` (additive optional `label:true` on `field()` → emits `<label for=id_…>`; existing callers unaffected). **Defect mechanism:** Django default `auto_id` renders `id="id_{prefix}-{n}-…"` / `<label for="id_…">`; the old `^`-anchored bare-prefix regex never matched past `id_`, so cloned/added rows carried DUPLICATE ids + labels whose `for=` pointed at row 0 (invalid HTML / a11y) on every JS-added row (the "+ Add" control + the F4/F5 multi-row assists). The assist PREFILL was unaffected (apply selects by `[name$=]`, and `name` was reindexed) — which is why it escaped until the DEC-31 behavioral harness. **Deliberately-broken probe (IMPL, local, reverted):** restoring the old regex made the 3 id/for/nested assertions FAIL, then the fix restored → all 27 pass — proving the tests guard the fix. **IMPL deviation (minor, test-side):** scoped the parent-clone selection to `:scope > .planner-fs-rows` because the bare descendant selector also reached nested rows boxes — a fixture-query correction, not a change to the fix or the `[name$=]` contract. **Orchestrator independent verify:** `make test-js` **27/27** (was 25); manage.py check 0; makemigrations --check NO changes; ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing; the 3 touched JS files add zero); ruff format clean (354); mypy **260**; pytest **1079** (unchanged — JS-only); commit clean, 0 node_modules tracked, tree clean. **No DEC** (a bugfix under DEC-31's harness); **no US-STATUS row**. The DEC-31 harness paid off immediately: it surfaced DISC-21 AND then proved its fix. Tracking: `phases/discoveries.md` DISC-21 `resolved_by` filled (`7217045`); STATE Known-issues DISC-21 OPEN→resolved + gate anchor re-anchored to `7217045`/27 tests + stamp; this line; tracking commit. Tree clean.

text: DEC-32 — **Guiding-principle atoms + stored principle-linkages carrying rationale (the accreditation-evidence model)**, enriching the DEC-29 orchestration grounding. User think-out across this turn; user initially said "don't record till I validate and verify" → I HELD (no edits) through ~6 iterative refinements, then user said "yes capture it in detail" → recorded now. **Design SETTLED for the atom/linkage core; NOT built.** Frame: a plan = current→desired transformation measured by success criteria, each division fostering purview-appropriate change; the LLM reasons over structured grounding atoms to decompose rationally; the resulting plan-element↔atom linkages each carry a `rationale` that IS the WASC committee's evidence (+ feeds the canonical materialization, DEC-18). **Atoms:** (1) **SLOs resolved to the canonical 7** (Engaged/Reflective/Compassionate/Confident/Innovative/Responsible/Bilingual Communicators) → a `LearnerOutcome` RESEED, NO schema change (Confident's description given; 6 OWED by user); (2) **Vision+Mission = first-class clause atoms** (a new kind=vision|mission prose+ordered-clause model, zh_hans first-class; mission statement given+atomized [umbrella+stance+6 aim-clauses]; verbatim VISION text OWED by user; SLO/mission overlap left un-reconciled as genre-inherent); (3) **Division responsibilities atomized** → new `DivisionResponsibility` model replacing the single `scope_summary` blob as the mappable units (atoms single-division-owned; "overlap" = concern-level co-recruitment, e.g. behavior → C&T via lessons + SAO via surveys, each via its OWN atoms). **Reasoning frame:** orienting atoms (vision/mission/SLO) × agency atoms (division responsibilities, purview-bounded) × the current→desired delta (by success criteria); recruitment INFERENTIAL (no concern-tag model — model supplies atoms, LLM supplies best-practice reasoning); output = editable prefill (model-is-contract). **Stored principle-linkages, each +rationale (uniform, mirroring A2's `PlanAccreditationStandard`):** WASC standards (EXISTS, A2); **SLOs → ENTAILED UPGRADE of A3's plain `learner_outcomes_targeted` M2M to a through-model+rationale**; mission_areas → same entailed upgrade; vision/mission clauses → NEW plan-level through-model; division responsibilities → NEW `ActionStepResponsibility` (step↔responsibility+rationale, M2M). **mandate-007 consequence surfaced:** the user's "rationale = accreditation evidence" principle makes A3's just-shipped plain SLO/mission-area M2Ms under-built → recorded as an entailed rationale-upgrade, not left silently inconsistent. **Baked:** division-coherence (step's fulfilled responsibility ∈ owner_division); ActionStepResponsibility M2M; principle-rationales OPTIONAL/non-breaking by default (gate-required later if user elects); EN-first rationale EXCEPT vision/mission content (zh_hans first-class). **Open (recorded):** which other A3 M2Ms get rationale (areas_for_improvement plausibly yes; stakeholder_impact/policies no — user decides); scope_summary fate; MissionArea-vocab vs mission-clauses; the grounding emitters + assist(s) that fill these; US stories to be authored when sequenced. **Cross-plan coherence** (the original message's other strand, LESS developed) recorded as a direction: draft-time detection of action-step/timeline/division-purview overlaps + timing/workflow conflicts vs the school's EXISTING active plans, surfaced on the draft-review surface (pull-flag per DEC-23/30, not push) — own modeling + stories when scoped. User think-out (continuation of the DEC-32 strand); recorded on "let's capture the whole orchestration/commenting/export strand." Design captured; NOT built. Refines DEC-29 from a single auto-shot into a **staged, human-checkpointed build**: US-LLM-24's heuristic runs as ordered STAGES (each = a reasoning pass the LLM drafts → admin reviews/edits → explicitly CLOSES → next stage runs grounded on the confirmed prior stages); admin is decider at EVERY stage boundary; whole artifact stays `proposed` until the single admin promotion (US-UI-6). **Corrected pass order: division-responsibility recruitment BEFORE milestones** (divisions = who/what-sphere; milestones = the time-phased arc organized over that work) — reasoning order ≠ storage order (no conflict; ActionStepResponsibility rows written at step-decomposition). Roles: US-LLM-23 = staged runner; US-LLM-24 = stage order/heuristic (corrected); US-LLM-25 = final gap-closure stage. **Stored drafting-stage sub-state DECIDED (option b, not UI-only)** — orthogonal to lifecycle (adds no lifecycle states); chosen stored because stage-comments + stage-export need a referenceable stage AND the long multi-pass checkpointed build must be resumable/auditable. **DEC-30 commenting extended:** stage becomes an added comment-target LEVEL ({plan, stage, element}) — additive, does NOT resolve DEC-30's open element-atomicity sub-question; enables an optional per-stage feedback loop at checkpoints (build act vs review act meet at the stage seam; advisory only). **NEW: draft/stage PDF export** (operationalizes DEC-18 for the draft phase) — admin exports the whole draft OR a specific stage as PDF via the canonical (or a lighter draft-review) template, for distribution+commentary; two scopes (whole + stage-scoped, the latter needs stored stages); template openness (canonical for committee / review-view for stakeholders; DraftDetailView a plausible HTML basis); mechanism OPEN + deploy-adjacent (WeasyPrint / headless-chrome / print-CSS). **Interlocks:** stored stages enable stage-comments + stage-export; export is the artifact the comment loop circulates; comments feed the admin's promotion. Rests on DEC-32's atom/linkage grounding → DEC-32 reference+linkage layers are a PRECONDITION for a rational US-LLM-24. So Layer 2b upgrades ALL SIX of A3's plain plan-level M2Ms to rationale-bearing through-models (mirroring `PlanAccreditationStandard`): `learner_outcomes_targeted`→`PlanLearnerOutcome`, `mission_areas_targeted`→`PlanMissionArea`, `areas_for_improvement`→`PlanAreaForImprovement`, `stakeholder_impact`→`PlanStakeholderImpact`, `policies_established`→`PlanPolicyEstablished`, `policies_revised`→`PlanPolicyRevised` — each + `rationale` (= the accreditation evidence; uniform with standards [A2] + SLO/mission/vision-clause). **Consequence:** A3 becomes a uniform ALL-FORMSET rationale assist (a larger A2); the `applyMultiSelectPrefill` client path + `preview="multi-select"` mode + the `jstests/apply-multi-select.test.mjs` (built solely for A3) RETIRE as dead code in 2b (test-js count will drop by those). text: DEC-32 **Layer 2b — all six A3 alignment M2Ms → rationale-bearing through-models + A3 apply reworked to formsets + multi-select retired** BUILT (`867be08`). User-directed "go on" (canonical process). Plan `~/.claude/plans/gleaming-skipping-emerson.md` (approved); explore=Explore agent (nailed the 6-way data-preserving migration recipe + the retire list + the all-formset apply); no user fork (the all-six scope was the user's prior decision; the rationale-PROPOSING is US-LLM-26, next — capture-then-assist split). text: DISC-22-0526-A — **responsibility is division-grained but responsibilities are role-borne**. Surfaced by the user during the DEC-32 build (the SAO org question → "positions can't carry atomic responsibilities ... the elaboration of possible responsibilities and roles ... can't be exactly articulated"). Two prongs: (1) `Assignment` has no `position` target (accountable party = division | optional person, no role rung) → SAO-pastoral steps collapse to "SAO" (no sub-divisions; YGL/HRT are Positions); (2) DEC-32's `DivisionResponsibility` atoms hang off Division ONLY → a Position (a future dorm-supervisor vs a YGL) cannot carry its own responsibility atoms → role-specific responsibilities within a division aren't articulable. The plan-decomposition (US-LLM-24) + the responsibility-fulfillment assist (US-LLM-27) reason over a too-coarse grain. RESOLUTION DIRECTION (shape OPEN): make responsibility position-grained as well as division-grained — `PositionResponsibility` (parallel to DivisionResponsibility) OR a generalized `Responsibility`(division-or-position owner); + `Assignment.position`; + extend the DEC-32 `ActionStepResponsibility` linkage + grounding to the position grain. Division-level atoms stay valid (office-wide purview) — they EXTEND, not waste. Faithful to DISC-16 (YGL/HRT deliberately positions, not units; does NOT require year-group/homeroom sub-divisions). **SEQUENCING: settle BEFORE US-LLM-27** (the responsibility-fulfillment assist must reason/link at the right grain) — i.e. this gates/precedes US-LLM-27 in the DEC-32 follow-on. Persisted: DISC-22 in `phases/discoveries.md` (OPEN) + this line + STATE open-discovery pointer. No code; analysis/record only — the model shape is the user's open decision (think-out, then a plan→impl). Connects to DEC-32 (revises the atom grain) + the earlier-mentioned `Assignment.position` rung (now subsumed here as prong 1).

text: DISC-22 shape NARROWED — **dedicated `PositionResponsibility`, NOT a polymorphic owner**. User rejected the "generalized `Responsibility` whose owner is a division OR a position" option: "Positions have responsibilities. Responsibilities don't stay the same for position" — i.e. responsibilities are owned by a position as a first-class thing, and a division-responsibility vs a position-responsibility are DIFFERENT kinds of thing (each position has its own distinct set), not one generic entity floating between owners. So the resolution shape is **two dedicated, parallel models**: `DivisionResponsibility` (built, Layer 1 — office-wide purview, UNTOUCHED) + a NEW `PositionResponsibility` (FK position, statement, order — each position's own atoms; dorm-supervisor's set ≠ YGL's set). Purely ADDITIVE (no rework of the division-level atoms). The rest of the DISC-22 resolution stands: `Assignment.position` (the role rung) + extend the DEC-32 `ActionStepResponsibility` linkage + grounding to the position grain; settle before US-LLM-27. (The polymorphic single-`Responsibility` option is OUT.) DISC-22 row stays OPEN/point-in-time (discoveries append-only); the narrowed shape is recorded here + in STATE. No code; the build is a later plan→impl (think-out continuing).

"Each division AND position can be and IS responsible for their part in a school-wide plan; the LLM (and humans) must know the 'divisions + positions + respective responsibilities INVENTORY' so it can be allocated to meet the plan's end-state criteria." The "inventory" = `DivisionResponsibility` (built) + the new `PositionResponsibility` atoms, BOTH emitted in grounding as the allocatable responsibility set; the DEC-32 `ActionStepResponsibility` linkage references EITHER grain's atom; US-LLM-24/27 allocate the inventory to steps to meet end-state criteria. So DISC-22's build (its own increment, before US-LLM-27): `PositionResponsibility` model + `Assignment.position` + the constraint rewrite (option i) + extend the linkage + grounding (emit positions + their atoms as the inventory) + admin + grant + migration + tests. text: DEC-35 — **HOD is modeled as a `Position` under the subject-department `Division`, complementary to `Division.head`.** Resolves the head-vs-position question surfaced after DISC-22. User: "HOD responsibilities are meant to be part of the allocatable inventory the orchestration reasons over (US-LLM-24/27); the HOD needs a Position representation under the subject-dept division, not only the head FK." Rationale: `Division.head` (FK→Person) is a bare org-leader pointer — no `PositionResponsibility` atoms, and `Assignment` has no "head" target (only division/position, DISC-22) → an HOD modeled only as `head` cannot carry role-specific responsibility atoms, cannot enter the DISC-22 division+position responsibility INVENTORY, and cannot be the responsible party on a step at the HOD grain. Decision: represent the HOD as a `Position` (a "Head of Department" seat) under each subject-dept `Division` (children of C&T), carrying `PositionResponsibility` atoms (= its job description, DEC-29) and `Assignment.position`-assignable so US-LLM-24/27 allocate it. COMPLEMENTARY not redundant ("not only the head FK"): `Division.head` retained as the rollup leader-pointer (`people_in_subtree`, `Person.heads`); the HOD `Position` is the responsibility-bearing seat; a dept leader is linked BOTH ways (head FK + a `Holding` of the HOD Position). **NO model change** — Position + PositionResponsibility + Assignment.position + grounding positions-nesting all exist (DISC-22 `a38c561`); the grounding inventory picks up the HOD automatically once the Position rows + atoms exist as data. Supersedes the `docs/domain-model.md` Position-entry prior art ("an HOD is not a position — it is the head"), corrected this turn. SCOPE narrow (the HOD/subject-dept case named); the same mechanism is already DISC-22-supported for any other division head, but that is the user's call when it arises (not prescribed). Follow-on build (own plan→impl; orchestrator-direct seed): a data migration creating an HOD `Position` under each of the 5 seeded subject departments, keyed (division,label) per the get_or_create seed convention; its responsibility atoms are admin-authored content (owed). Seed details to settle at seed time (content, not structure): the label convention (uniform "Head of Department" vs per-subject) + whether atoms are a shared role description or per-dept. People-linking (head FK + HOD Holding) needs the roster — D7-blocked; the Position+atoms seed independently. No new US story minted (mechanism + admin editing already exist; no augmentation). Persisted: DEC-35 (data/seed-round-plan.md) + docs/domain-model.md (Position entry + Division.head note) + this line + STATE.

text: `docs/hod-default-responsibilities.md` created — the HOD "goes-without-saying" default responsibility set DERIVED from the modeled universe (only `ai/services/grounding.py` sections; no external JD lore), elaborating DEC-35. Hard finding recorded: defaults must be MATERIALIZED stored atoms (not computed at grounding time) because DEC-32's evidence linkage (`ActionStepPositionResponsibility.position_responsibility` FK → a real `PositionResponsibility` row) cannot point at a derived string — so pure-derived is disqualified for anything the orchestration allocates. Content split across the two DISC-22 grains: DIVISION-grain `DivisionResponsibility` defaults (curriculum/teaching delivery in {subject} ← Division-under-C&T + ImprovementType; assess learning + evidence ← SuccessCriterion/Measurement/Evidence; maintain dept policies ← Policy division-owned; incorporate subject feedback ← StakeholderGroup/FeedbackChannel) + POSITION-grain `PositionResponsibility` defaults (lead/develop staff ← Position/Holding/people_in_subtree + PD-Leader seat; accountable for the division's atoms ← Division.head + DivisionResponsibility; own dept plans + be the responsible party ← Plan author + Assignment(responsible=position); represent dept in review/revision ← ReviewEvent/RevisionRule; curate accreditation evidence ← AccreditationStandard + rationale linkages). EXCLUSION recorded: do NOT re-store SLOs/standards/vision-mission/mission-areas/AFIs as responsibility atoms — already first-class with plan-grain rationale linkages (PlanLearnerOutcome/PlanAccreditationStandard/PlanGuidingClause/PlanMissionArea/PlanAreaForImprovement); the HOD's relation to them is the generic "ensure the dept advances them," already carried. Mechanism: canonical defaults defined once, idempotently seeded per subject dept onto Division + HOD Position, parameterized by subject, per-dept overridable (DISC-22) — resolves DEC-35's shared-vs-per-dept sub-question as "shared defaults, per-dept overridable" (pending validation). THREE open forks: (1) how the system recognizes an HOD position [label-convention vs structural-via-head vs a role/type marker — load-bearing; bears on DEC-35's "no model change"]; (2) default-text evolution [get_or_create won't update existing rows — needs an update migration/mgmt command that won't clobber admin-edited rows]; (3) the grain split itself. Substance + forks NOT yet user-validated. References wired (per user "reference it in persistent for future focus"): DEC-35 (seed-round-plan.md) cross-ref + ORCHESTRATOR-STATE.md DEC-35 bullet pointer. On validation → a DEC (likely extending DEC-35) + the materialization seed as its follow-on plan→impl, fork 1 first.

text: **responsibility-fulfillment assist built — per action step, which responsibility atom(s) it fulfills + rationale, in BOTH DISC-22 grains.** Realizes DISC-22's purpose (the division+position responsibility inventory feeding the orchestration). One assist (`propose-responsibilities`, target_step="steps", preview="responsibilities", grounding_include=("school","divisions","draft_state"), resolve present, label "Map responsibilities fulfilled"). Contract = per-step, two nested grain lists (F5 doubled): `{"steps":[{"step_index":int, "division_responsibilities":[{division,statement,rationale}], "position_responsibilities":[{division,position,statement,rationale}]}]}`. `steps` non-empty; both grain lists OPTIONAL (default [] — responsibility-fulfillment is NOT gate-required); per-row `statement`+owner strict, `rationale` lenient (missing→""); EN-only. `resolve_responsibilities_vocab` maps statement+owner→school-scoped pk (division-grain via `DivisionResponsibility.objects.filter(division__school=…, division__label=…, statement=…)`; position-grain via `PositionResponsibility.objects.filter(position__division__school=…, position__division__label=…, position__label=…, statement=…)`), keeps `{pk,rationale}`, DROPS unresolvable (LLM never sees pks). Client `applyResponsibilitiesPrefill` (preview "responsibilities") navigates per step to BOTH nested formsets (`stepChildFormset` segments "responsibility" + "position_responsibility"), grows via addRows, fills each row's FK select + the EN `-rationale` field. Seed `ai/0017_seed_propose_responsibilities_template` (NEW template via get_or_create — correct for a new template; no-op reverse). Soft coherence by PROMPT only (prefer atoms owned by the step's responsible unit; through-model clean() + human review the backstops), NOT hard-coupled to F2. UNTOUCHED: the through-models/forms/formsets/`ACTIONSTEP_CHILD_FORMSETS`/per-step capture+render, grounding, gate, schema; NO plans/school migration. **Gate re-run independent (orchestrator):** check 0; makemigrations No changes; ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing, zero new); ruff format 392 clean; mypy Success 282; pytest 1431 passed; make test-js 42 pass/0 fail. **Dev DB:** ai/0017 applied + verified (template carries the two-grain contract; with temp DivisionResponsibility + PositionResponsibility atoms, parse read both grains, resolve produced `{pk,rationale}` matching the temp atoms, dropped an unknown statement, school-scoped; temp atoms rolled back). **Minor IMPL deviation (benign):** the IMPL subagent applied ai/0017 to the dev DB during its run (dev-DB application is normally orchestrator-only) — idempotent + independently re-verified correct; noted, no remediation needed. US-STATUS US-LLM-27 → enabled (`622aa35`); row description updated to the as-built two-grain scope (DISC-22). **The per-element evidence assist layer is now COMPLETE (US-LLM-26 + US-LLM-27).** NEXT FOCUS = the orchestration trio (US-LLM-24 dependency-aware sequencing heuristic → US-LLM-25 gap-closure → US-LLM-23 staged whole-draft) + stored drafting-stage substate + US-UI-6 (admin promote).

text: `docs/tenant-vs-generic-cleavage.md` created from the 2026-05-26 think-out responding to "how to cleanly separate (1) the current Chiway-focused seeded-school app from (2) a possible generalized multi-tenant school-planning app without opinionated/alien naming heuristics." Substance verbatim (no augmentation). Core findings recorded: the model layer is already ~90% generic (every school-scoped row carries a `school` FK; tenant content lives in seed migrations scoped to `chiway-repton-xiamen`); THREE places where tenant opinion bleeds past the data layer — (1) `Person.name` is a single translated field but the CSV exposes a three-part Latin name + missing native-script field (locale-neutral remedy: `family_name`/`given_name`/`display_name`/`name_native[+_zh_hans/_ko/…]`); (2) year groups are unmodeled but tenant data implies them (remedy: a generic `YearGroup` model + tenant labels); (3) DEC-35's HOD-as-Position pattern is stated narrowly for subject-dept but the CSV shows it applies to every leadership unit (Director of AAO/SAO/T&C/Innovation Center/IHS Principal/etc.) — generic remedy is to extend DEC-35 to "any leadership unit." Cleavage recommendation: two layers — Layer A (current apps: generic model + global reference, no new tenant seeds going forward) + Layer B (a NEW `tenant_chiway` app, zero models, data migrations only; second tenant = a parallel `tenant_xxx` app added to INSTALLED_APPS); existing `school/0006–0042` historical migrations stay in place (immutable). Carry-forward list (5 items, condensed): (1) locale-neutral Person name structure; (2) DEC-35 generalization; (3) `YearGroup` model; (4) `tenant_chiway` app — first migration = the Dorm Supervisor seed already owed; (5) optional later — `AccreditationBody` parent / promote planning recipes to a global curated library. text: **Locale-neutral `Person` name structure shipped, with project-wide modeltranslation fallback fix as the dev-DB-verify-exposed follow-on.** Two code commits this work-block:
continuation: - `51ff5cd` (Person refactor): replaced `Person.name` (single translated CharField) with four locale-neutral fields — `family_name` / `given_name` / `display_name` (all Latin, untranslated) + `name_native` (translated; `_en` + `_zh_hans` columns). New `__str__` fallback chain: `display_name` → `f"{given_name} {family_name}".strip()` → `name_native` → `f"Person #{pk}"`. New `Meta.ordering = ["family_name", "given_name"]`. `PersonAdmin.list_display` / `search_fields` updated. Translation registration swap (drop `name`, add `name_native`). One schema migration `school/0043_person_name_refactor` (6 AddField + 3 RemoveField + 1 AlterModelOptions; reversible; safe — 0 Person rows on dev DB). ~30 test-fixture callsites mechanically updated (`name="X Y"` → `family_name="Y", given_name="X"` pattern); new positive coverage on `__str__` fallback chain + translation round-trip + ordering. `User` auth model UNTOUCHED (separate scope). No business code reads `Person.name` directly (only `__str__`); AI grounding does not emit Person names; no plans/school FK changes needed.

- `363454f` (project-wide fallback fix): dev-DB verification of `51ff5cd` exposed a defect — `Person.__str__`'s `self.name_native` read is the modeltranslation **active-language proxy**, which returns empty when only `_zh_hans` has content under EN-active context (default), so a Chinese-only Person rendered `"Person #N"` instead of `"方苑苑"`. **Project-wide audit (Explore) found the SAME bug pattern in 32 of 47 `__str__` methods** across school + plans (Position, Division, DivisionResponsibility, Plan, Phase, Milestone, ActionStep, SubStep, Communication, Evidence, FeedbackChannel, ReviewEvent, RevisionRule, SuccessCriterion, StatementClause, every reference-vocab label-bearing model, School.name itself). Root cause: no `MODELTRANSLATION_FALLBACK_LANGUAGES` setting → library defaults to no auto-fallback. Fix: ONE config line in `config/settings/base.py` — `MODELTRANSLATION_FALLBACK_LANGUAGES = ("en", "zh-hans")`. Bidirectional symmetric cascade (active-language column first, then walk tuple in order until non-empty). Read-only — no save behavior change, no `.filter()` change. **Per mandate-004 a Person-only fix would have been negligent** (31 other models silently broken when the root-cause fix is one line). Tests: simplified the existing `test_str_falls_back_to_name_native_when_latin_blank` to drop the workaround that set both base + `_zh_hans` (now sets only `_zh_hans` and asserts surfacing); new `test_str_falls_back_to_name_native_when_chinese_active` proves the symmetric reverse under `override("zh-hans")`; new `school/tests/test_translation_fallback.py` proves the fix is project-wide via Position + DivisionResponsibility regression assertions. No `__str__` code change in any of the 32 sites — the settings fix obsoletes per-model workarounds.

**Process notes (mandate-007 / -004 transparency):** the original `51ff5cd` test passed because it used the base `name_native=` field directly (which under EN-active populates both `_en` and the base) — masking the bug; the dev-DB probe under realistic per-language-column authoring exposed it. The fix path was decided by Explore audit (32 affected sites) → root cause (one config line) rather than localized patching. **One process error this work-block:** the IMPL subagent for `51ff5cd` applied `school/0043` to the dev DB during its run (orchestrator territory; idempotent + verified). Also: a transient AskUserQuestion was sent to the user that proliferated options when the path was settled (saved as memory `feedback-options-proliferation-noise`); recovered to single-path plan.

**Gate re-run independent (orchestrator):** check 0; makemigrations No changes; ruff baseline-only (6 pre-existing, zero new across both commits); ruff format 394 clean; mypy Success **283 source files** (+1 from `test_translation_fallback.py`); pytest **1438 passed** (+7 vs. US-LLM-27's 1431 baseline: 4 new from `51ff5cd`'s test_person.py expansions, 1 chinese-active fallback test, 2 project-wide regression tests); make test-js **42** unchanged. **Dev DB:** `school/0043` applied + verified — six new cols present (`family_name`, `given_name`, `display_name`, `name_native`, `name_native_en`, `name_native_zh_hans`), three old cols dropped (`name`, `name_en`, `name_zh_hans`); Person `__str__` 4-path round-trip now ALL correct (path 1 display=`Celestine`; path 2 given+family=`Yuanyuan Fang`; path 3 zh_hans-only=`方苑苑`; path 4 pk-defensive=`Person #N`); Position `label_zh_hans`-only renders `数学教师 (...)`; DivisionResponsibility `statement_zh_hans`-only renders `按时检查作业`. **Cleavage carry-forward item 1 (`docs/tenant-vs-generic-cleavage.md`) → BUILT + VERIFIED.** Items 2–5 (DEC-35 generalization, YearGroup model, `tenant_chiway` app, optional AccreditationBody parent) remain pending user validation. **Working-tree note:** `data/school-content-intake-2026-05-26.md` carries uncommitted user edits + the staff roster CSV `data/may-27-2026-staff-names-crs.csv` remains untracked — both user-owned working surfaces, not orchestrator-committable.
