type: Settled + persisted DEC-57: the production orchestration-trio architecture. After a forensic state investigation (4 read-only agents over git + claude-history + live source) established the project's real gap is small and clear -- build the trio (US-LLM-23/24/25) + close 3 pending assists (US-LLM-7/10/15) -- and a current-practice research pass (deep-research workflow, Mar-Jun 2026 sources, rate-limit-degraded to 10/25 verified) endorsed the lean path, the architecture was recorded as DEC-57 + a referenceable doc docs/production-orchestration-trio-architecture-2026-06-20.md. Core: the trio is a server-side loop over the 14 dependency-ordered assists built in planner/plans/ai, reusing the 9 already-built essential families (build_grounding, the 14 parse_*+_check_in_catalogue, PromptTemplate+ai/migrations, AssistStreamView, _check_promotion_gates, save_nested, advance_plan_status -- all file:line verified), importing ZERO prompt-workshop/pi-workflows/.workshopping code (verified: only docstrings+tests reference the workshop). The byzantine layer (pi-workflows+run_workflow.mjs, the substrate, dual-render, verify-render-parity, check_draft.py 1158, verify-citability.py 913, self_check) is deliberately off-path (29 filed toolchain defects) and retired after the trio; its legitimate kernel (real grounding+parsers vs real tenant data) is value already delivered. Six research-grounded design decisions (decompose+reason-then-format; grammar-constrained shape + parsers for meaning; catalogue-anchored generation w/ provenance; resilience via external-validator + bounded escalating repair + machine-readable feedback -> degrade-to-flag no aborts; deterministic gate not LLM-judge; plain loop not durable-execution unless heavy). The single genuinely-new piece is the server-side draft-state accumulation seam (production has no prefill->draft merge; the browser does it today). OPEN to plan-mode (not settled): accumulation seam Option A in-memory-merge vs Option B persist-as-you-go-via-save_nested; the per-spec retry/degrade policy; plain-loop vs durable-execution. NOTE: seq-6 focus item still points at the prompt-workshop vivify arc; the focus has not yet been flipped to the trio -- that refresh + the .context task filing happen at pre-impl when the build starts.

Byzantine layer off-path + retired. 6 Mar-Jun-2026-research-grounded decisions; one new piece = the draft-state accumulation seam. OPEN to plan-mode: seam A vs B, retry/degrade policy, plain-loop vs durable-exec. Followed the forensic investigation + the rate-limit-degraded deep-research run (10/25 verified). seq-6 focus NOT yet flipped to the trio (happens at pre-impl).

type: Resolved DEC-57's three 'open forks' into one settled design after the human challenged whether they were truly open or had a best-practice answer. Three facts verified vs live source decided them: (a) Plans persist ONLY via one atomic whole-graph POST (PlanCreateView transaction.atomic + save_nested; formsets.py:470 assumes a saved Plan + open transaction) -- no partial-Plan persist exists; (b) _check_promotion_gates runs at PROMOTE-time on a SAVED proposed Plan (it does .exists()/.all() on related managers, needs a PK), and saving a proposed Plan is gate-free; (c) the FlagSeverity (MUST_FIX vs soft) system already exists. Resolution: (1) cross-stage accumulation = a PERSISTED flat draft_state row (DEC-33 needs the draft to survive admin review between stages; a partial Plan cannot persist), folded per-spec via the inverse of applyFormsetPrefill -- NOT the workshop MERGE_RULES (FGAP-018 bug source), NOT a partial Plan (so the earlier Option-A-vs-B framing is moot); (2) final persist = one atomic save_nested writing the whole proposed Plan, then the REAL gate on the saved Plan drives the US-LLM-25 closure loop (no parallel gate reimplementation); (3) per-spec resilience = bounded retry + machine-readable feedback then degrade-to-Flag, R~=2-3 a tuning constant; (4) plain loop, not Temporal/DBOS -- each stage is a discrete request over the DB draft row, no long-running in-process workflow to make durable; the DB row is the durability. Residual plan-mode items are verifiable facts, not forks: the draft-row's shape/location + save-then-gate-closure idempotency. Updated doc section 7 (now 'Resolved design') + amended DEC-57 body+raw to match.
raw: 2026-06-20 resolved DEC-57's 3 'open forks' to a settled design (human challenge: best practice exists). Decided by 3 live-source facts: atomic-only Plan persist, promote-time gate on a saved Plan, existing FlagSeverity. -> accumulation = persisted flat draft_state row (not partial-Plan, not workshop MERGE_RULES); final = one atomic save_nested then real gate drives the closure loop; resilience = bounded-retry+structured-feedback then degrade-to-Flag (R~=2-3 tuning); plain loop not durable-exec. Residual = verifiable facts not forks. Updated doc s7 + amended DEC-57.

type: TASK-067 DONE -- trio RESILIENCE layer (FEAT-010 slice 4, planner/orchestration.py): run_trio_pass now wraps each spec in a bounded retry/degrade loop (R=2) so a per-spec failure NEVER aborts the 14-spec run. Classification: transient LLM (LLMTimeoutError/LLMRateLimitError) retry same prompt; parse/catalogue/resolve ValueError retry WITH FEEDBACK (the rejection message appended as steering to the re-rendered prompt); LLMAuthenticationError + seam-fold TypeError/ValueError (via a _FoldError marker distinguishing fold from parse) degrade IMMEDIATELY. After exhaustion/non-retryable, the spec degrades to a recorded DegradedSpec (spec_key, attempts, reason, severity=FlagSeverity MUST_FIX value) and the loop continues; the degraded spec folds against a COPY (contributes nothing) and appends no version. run_trio_pass returns a 4th value = the DegradedSpec list. Cascade-tolerant (a degraded prior spec doesn't crash a later one). NO Flag-model-row + NO _check_promotion_gates call (slices #9/#8). Built + machine-verified through the harness (SLICE=resilience). type: TASK-070 DONE -- the gate-closure loop (FEAT-010 slice 8b, US-LLM-25). planner/orchestration.run_gate_closure_loop iterates the trio's accumulated draft_state to a Plan that PASSES _check_promotion_gates: per round it PROBES (save_draft_state_as_plan inside transaction.atomic + a _ProbeRollbackError sentinel so the probe persists NOTHING -- save is create-only), reads the gate's unmet ValidationError messages, maps each to its producing spec (_GATE_FAILURE_SPEC_RULES / _map_failures_to_specs), re-invokes those specs via _run_one_spec against the current draft_state + re-folds (REPLACE-scoped) in TRIO_SEQUENCE order (C1 before B2), re-probes; on a passing probe it does ONE real committed save -> the gate-passing Plan(lifecycle=PROPOSED); on cap-exhaustion (DEFAULT_GATE_CLOSURE_MAX_ROUNDS=4) or an all-residual round it returns (None, draft_state, residual) -- no raise, no unbounded loop. type: FGAP-051 filed -- 2nd live run_trio finding: an LLM model-field-constraint violation (label > max_length=200 from a feedback/milestone/review spec) aborts the WHOLE run at the terminal save. FGAP-050 confirmed fixed (no validator WARNINGs). Root cause: parsers validate shape+catalogue not field max_length -> over-long label folds into draft_state -> every gate-closure probe-save fails form validation -> loop never converges (plan=None) -> the fallback save_draft_state_as_plan at orchestration.py:1170 (NOT try/wrapped) raises -> CommandError, no Plan. Defeats no-run-aborts (DEC-57 dec-4) + always-yields-a-Plan (TASK-071); the resilience loop only covers per-spec failures, not the terminal save. CLASS: any CharField max_length / choices / constraint a spec fills. Fix direction (plan-mode next): make the terminal save ABORT-PROOF (degrade/flag constraint-violating rows, not raise) as the structural guarantee per mandate-004, + parser-level field-constraint validation for early retry-with-feedback.
